name: Asian Stir-Fry Topper
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine the broth, soy sauce, cornstarch, grated ginger, peanut butter, and crushed red pepper flakes. Mix until the cornstarch is dissolved. Set aside.
2. In a large nonstick skillet, heat the sesame oil over medium heat. Add the steak and cook, stirring frequently, until the beef is no longer pink and browned, about 3 to 4 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, remove the beef from the skillet and set aside.
3. Add the snow peas, scallions, and sesame seeds to the skillet; stir fry until the vegetables are crisp-tender, about 2 to 3 minutes.
4. Return the beef to the skillet. Stir in the broth mixture, and cook until the meat and vegetables are hot and the sauce has thickened, about 1 to 2 minutes.
5. Cut open the baked potatoes and top them with the beef-vegetable mixture.
